preset_description: Dark = identity signal. Visual language expresses who the user is. Validator gets authority and weight (heavy dark). Mover gets minimal light with one dark CTA punch. Explorer gets all-neutral — open, spacious, no urgency.

brief.audience: Startups needing an AI-ready design system

accent_tokens._note: Brand primary CTA color. Global — not per-preset. Two variants: button on a light/neutral section vs. on a dark section.

beats.name: Orientation
beats.rationale: Decision-stage rule requires Orientation first. Uses with-price Hero variant to surface the offer (price + CTA) above the fold, giving the Mover archetype immediate context and a fast-track commitment option before any proof sections.

beats.name: Credibility
beats.rationale: Decision-stage mandates Credibility before Conversion. For startups evaluating an AI-ready design system, a brief trust bar (logos, partner badges) provides just enough social proof without slowing the Mover's direct path.

beats.name: Value
beats.rationale: Decision-stage requires Value before Conversion. A concise capabilities section highlights the AI-ready differentiators that matter to startups — speed, adaptability, developer experience — reinforcing the pricing commitment shown in the Hero.

beats.name: Evidence
beats.rationale: Decision-stage rule requires Evidence before Conversion. Kept lean per Mover guidance: one or two sharp metrics (e.g., time-to-ship reduction, adoption stats) that validate the value claims and justify the price.

beats.name: Decision
beats.rationale: Decision-stage rule requires this beat before Conversion. Presents plan comparison and pricing details so the startup can make a confident, informed choice — completing the proof-to-purchase narrative.

beats.name: Conversion
beats.rationale: Must appear immediately before Recovery per system rules. Clear, singular CTA to convert to paid — no ambiguity, matching the Mover's preference for a direct commitment prompt.

beats.name: Recovery
beats.rationale: Required as the final beat. Provides secondary paths (contact sales, explore docs, FAQ) and footer for visitors who aren't ready to commit on this visit.

sections.name: Navigation
sections.rationale: Global navigation — wayfinding and persistent CTA for the session.

sections.name: Hero
sections.rationale: Decision-stage rule requires Orientation first. Uses with-price Hero variant to surface the offer (price + CTA) above the fold, giving the Mover archetype immediate context and a fast-track commitment option before any proof sections.

sections.name: Social proof
sections.rationale: Decision-stage mandates Credibility before Conversion. For startups evaluating an AI-ready design system, a brief trust bar (logos, partner badges) provides just enough social proof without slowing the Mover's direct path.

sections.name: Proof points
sections.rationale: Decision-stage rule requires Evidence before Conversion. Kept lean per Mover guidance: one or two sharp metrics (e.g., time-to-ship reduction, adoption stats) that validate the value claims and justify the price.

sections.name: Pricing
sections.rationale: Decision-stage rule requires this beat before Conversion. Presents plan comparison and pricing details so the startup can make a confident, informed choice — completing the proof-to-purchase narrative.

sections.name: CTA
sections.rationale: Must appear immediately before Recovery per system rules. Clear, singular CTA to convert to paid — no ambiguity, matching the Mover's preference for a direct commitment prompt.

sections.name: Footer
sections.rationale: Page close — brand mark, minimal navigation, and legal.

page.reasoning: Mover archetype on conversion funnel stage requires minimal variant — 'minimal variant for conversion-focused pages — strip nav_links, keep logo and single CTA only' aligns with decision-stage goal to convert to paid without navigation distraction.

Conversion funnel stage (decision) demands text-only variant: 'text-only for conversion: slim title and subhead only, no media, no secondary CTA — orients the visitor without competing with the decision moment below.' This prevents hero from competing with pricing and conversion actions.

slots.headline: Your design system doesn't know why.
slots.subhead: MODE resolves component behavior, visual emphasis, and copy register — automatically.
slots.trust_signal: One-time purchase. Lifetime access. No subscription.

page.reasoning: Mover archetype preference: 'Mover variant is logos-only, no headline — silent credibility' establishes trust without narrative overhead, fitting the Mover's efficiency-focused profile.

slots.headline: Built for teams that ship in code

page.reasoning: Mover archetype on decision stage: 'Mover variant suppresses sources and headline; pure metric impact. 3-stat is the preferred default.' Delivers impact metrics without attribution noise, accelerating decision-making.

stats.label: Components fully specified out of the box

page.reasoning: Mover archetype variant: 'Mover variant strips description; price and CTA only' with standard base supports lean, focused pricing presentation aligned with Mover efficiency expectations on a conversion page.

slots.description: The full intelligence layer. Yours forever.
features: Next.js preview UI with overlay rendering
Bring your own API key — no vendor billing

page.reasoning: Mover archetype: 'Mover variant: strong headline, single CTA, no subhead. minimal for high-frequency use — above footer, between sections' provides focused conversion moment without persuasion friction.

slots.headline: Ship smarter. Own the stack.
slots.subhead: No subscription. No lock-in. Running in under an hour.
slots.trust_signal: Lifetime access. Fork it, extend it, make it yours.

page.reasoning: Decision-stage conversion page requires 'with-cta variant recaptures departing users on key conversion pages — use on pricing and signup flows' to recover users reaching page bottom without converting.
